# Development Workflow

## Purpose

This document defines the standard development workflow for Praxis360.

For the complete development methodology, see:

- Docs/AIPlaybook.md
- Docs/SprintBook.md

---

## General Workflow

Every development task follows the same workflow.

1. Understand the request.
2. Read the relevant documentation.
3. Review the existing implementation.
4. Design the solution.
5. Implement the feature.
6. Compile the project.
7. Review the result.
8. Update documentation if required.
9. Commit the changes.
10. Push to GitHub.

---

## Sprint Workflow

Every Sprint must:

- Deliver one complete feature.
- Compile successfully.
- Respect the architecture.
- Respect the Design System.
- Keep documentation synchronized.

Never start a new Sprint before the current Sprint is fully completed.

---

## Documentation Workflow

Before considering a Sprint complete:

- Update Blueprint if required.
- Update ProductBook if functionality changed.
- Update Architecture if technical structure changed.
- Update DesignBible if UI changed.
- Update MotionGuide if animations changed.
- Update SprintBook.
- Update Roadmap if long-term planning changed.

Documentation is part of the product.

---

## Quality Checklist

Before committing:

- Project compiles.
- No duplicated code.
- Existing functionality preserved.
- Existing components reused.
- Documentation updated.
- Naming conventions respected.

---
